title: cereus divaricatus
intro: Cereus divaricatus, also known as the Devil's head cactus, is a species of cactus found in the deserts of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Cereus divaricatus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, salves, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
